points.text: Common symptoms of COVID-19 include cough, fever, and loss of smell and taste.
Other symptoms may include fatigue, breathing difficulties, muscle pain, sore throat, and diarrhea.
Loss of taste and smell can occur in up to 88% of symptomatic cases without prior ear, nose, or throat disorders.
Symptoms can vary based on the variant and may change over time, ranging from mild to severe.
In severe cases, symptoms may include shortness of breath and require hospitalization.
Common symptoms include dry cough, shortness of breath, fatigue, and loss of taste or smell.
Other symptoms may include fever, chills, headaches, body aches, and digestive issues like upset stomach or diarrhea.
Symptoms can appear 2 to 14 days after exposure to the virus.
Severe symptoms requiring emergency help include trouble breathing, persistent chest pain, and new confusion.
Some individuals may experience symptoms worsening around 5 to 7 days after onset.
The most common symptoms of COVID-19 include fever, cough, and tiredness.
Other symptoms can include shortness of breath, chest pain, muscle aches, headache, and loss of taste or smell.
Additional symptoms may include confusion, sore throat, congestion, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, and skin rashes.
Infants may experience difficulty feeding as a symptom of COVID-19.
Children generally experience similar symptoms but tend to have less severe illness compared to adults.
